## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On December 21, 1988, an incredibly massive, absolutely horrifying, and historically definitive act of international terrorism violently occurred. Pan Am Flight 103, a massive Boeing 747 en route to New York, was aggressively obliterated by a plastic explosive bomb over Lockerbie, Scotland. The incredibly massive explosion resulted in the horrific deaths of all 259 people on board and 11 on the ground. This massive atrocity fundamentally and aggressively altered global aviation security protocols and triggered an incredibly massive, decade-long diplomatic confrontation with Libya.

On December 21, 1968, the incredibly massive, universally celebrated, and absolutely historic Apollo 8 mission was aggressively and successfully launched from Cape Kennedy. Heavily carrying astronauts Frank Borman, Jim Lovell, and William Anders, this massive mission marked the absolute, very first time human beings aggressively left Earth's orbit and traveled to the Moon. Their incredibly massive, stunning "Earthrise" photograph fundamentally and absolutely revolutionized massive global environmental consciousness, demonstrating the absolute, fragile beauty of the planet.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On December 21, 1914, the incredibly massive, highly strategic, and historically defining First Battle of Givenchy aggressively commenced during World War I. The massive Indian Corps, heavily comprising incredibly brave sepoys from the 3rd (Lahore) and 7th (Meerut) divisions, fiercely and aggressively engaged in brutal trench warfare against the German Imperial Army in France. Their incredibly massive, uncompromising heroism under horrific conditions fundamentally and absolutely secured the Allied lines during the "Race to the Sea," cementing the massive reputation of Indian soldiers on the global stage.

On December 21, 1952, the incredibly massive, highly significant, and absolutely foundational first session of the Indian National Health Council was officially and aggressively held. Heavily designed by the newly independent republic to aggressively coordinate massive, nationwide public health policies, this monumental event fundamentally laid the absolute, architectural bedrock for India's massive rural healthcare infrastructure and aggressive vaccination programs, fiercely aiming to absolutely eradicate the massive, paralyzing diseases that plagued the deeply impoverished populace.
